title: E-Commerce Shopping Cart with Checkout
spec:
## Requirements

### R1: Cart Management
Add/remove/update items in a shopping cart. Cart persists across sessions via PostgreSQL. Each cart item references a product SKU with quantity.

### R2: Price Calculation
Compute cart total: sum of (item price × quantity) for all items, minus applicable discounts, plus tax. Tax rate depends on shipping destination. Discounts can be percentage or fixed amount. Multiple discounts can stack.

### R3: Inventory Reservation
When checkout begins, reserve inventory for all cart items atomically. If any item is out of stock, the entire reservation fails. Reserved inventory has a 15-minute TTL — if checkout doesn't complete, reservation expires and inventory is released.

### R4: Payment Processing
Charge the customer via Stripe. Support credit card and ACH. Payment must be atomic with order creation — if payment fails, no order is created and inventory is released.

### R5: Order Creation
Create an order record with all line items, applied discounts, tax, shipping, and payment confirmation. Order creation, inventory deduction, and payment capture must be a single atomic transaction.

### R6: Notifications
Send order confirmation email via SendGrid. Send low-stock alert to admin when inventory drops below threshold.

## Data Flow
CartItem[] → PriceCalculation → CartTotal
CartTotal + ShippingAddress → TaxCalculation → OrderTotal
OrderTotal → InventoryReservation (atomic)
InventoryReservation + PaymentIntent → PaymentCapture (atomic)
PaymentCapture + InventoryDeduction + OrderRecord → OrderConfirmation (ALL ATOMIC)

## Build Sequence
1. R1 (Cart) — foundation
2. R2 (Pricing) — depends on R1
3. R3 (Inventory) — depends on R1
4. R4 + R5 (Payment + Order) — MUST be built together (atomic transaction)
5. R6 (Notifications) — depends on R5

## Architecture
PostgreSQL for carts, orders, inventory. Redis for inventory reservation TTL. Stripe API for payments. SendGrid for emails.
rejection:
## Anti-Patterns

### Do NOT separate order creation from payment capture
These are ONE atomic transaction. If they're in different agents or different tools without shared transaction context, you WILL get orphan orders (paid but not recorded) or orphan charges (recorded but not paid).

### Do NOT compute price in the frontend
All price calculation must happen server-side. The cart stores quantities and SKUs, not prices. Prices are looked up at calculation time.

### Do NOT reserve inventory without TTL
Reservations without expiry create permanent inventory locks on abandoned checkouts.

### Do NOT send notifications synchronously
Notifications are events, not commands. Checkout must not wait for email delivery.

## Boundaries
- Price calculation is pure domain logic — no I/O, no side effects
- Inventory reservation is a command with rollback capability
- Payment + Order + Inventory Deduction = single atomic transaction (hyperedge)
- Notifications are fire-and-forget events
- Tax calculation is a separate concern from price calculation (different rules, different update frequency)

## Drift Risks
- Pricing logic creeping into cart management (cart stores data, pricing computes)
- Payment and order becoming separate non-atomic operations
- Inventory check (query) being confused with inventory reservation (command)
additionalConstraints: The order+payment+inventory transaction MUST be atomic
